_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Bumiputera Contract Quota System Loses RM12B to Rent-Seeking
context: A MACC investigation revealed that an estimated RM12 billion annually in government procurement value is lost to Ali Baba arrangements where Bumiputera quota holders sell contracts to non-Bumiputera operators for 5-15% commissions.

cards.text: RM12 billion yearly. Bumiputera contract quotas create a commission economy where middlemen profit without doing any work.
cards.sub: Ali Baba practice is open secret: 34% of surveyed contractors admitted participating in MACC's 2025 study

cards.h: Government procurement at RM150 billion annually allocates 30% or RM45 billion to Bumiputera-only contracts
cards.s: If RM12 billion leaks to rent-seeking, effective Bumiputera benefit is only RM33 billion, a 27% loss to middlemen
cards.d: Government procurement at RM150 billion annually allocates 30% or RM45 billion to Bumiputera-only contracts If RM12 billion leaks to rent-seeking, effective Bumiputera benefit is only RM33 billion, a 27% loss to middlemen

cards.h: Genuine Bumiputera contractors compete against commission-seekers who undercut on price then subcontract to lowest bidder
cards.s: Quality suffers: Bumiputera-quota projects show 28% higher defect rates than open-tender projects per JKR data
cards.d: Genuine Bumiputera contractors compete against commission-seekers who undercut on price then subcontract to lowest bidder Quality suffers: Bumiputera-quota projects show 28% higher defect rates than open-tender projects per JKR data

cards.h: MACC prosecuted only 42 Ali Baba cases in 2025 despite estimating 8,000-12,000 such arrangements exist nationwide
cards.s: Contract monitoring ends at award stage. No system tracks whether the awarded Bumiputera company actually performs the work.
cards.d: MACC prosecuted only 42 Ali Baba cases in 2025 despite estimating 8,000-12,000 such arrangements exist nationwide Contract monitoring ends at award stage. No system tracks whether the awarded Bumiputera company actually performs the work.

cards.text: A quota system that makes more money for middlemen than for actual Bumiputera businesses defeats its own purpose
Like a fishing quota where most of the catch goes to the boat broker, not the fisherman. The policy feeds the middleman, not the intended beneficiary.
cards.sub: Ali Baba arrangements redirect procurement value from Bumiputera businesses to rent-seeking intermediaries

cards.text: Should Bumiputera procurement quotas shift from company ownership to actual workforce composition and capability metrics?

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
